name: Apteva Apps
description: Curated registry of Apteva Apps. Each entry points at a manifest URL the platform can fetch + install.

apps.name: simple
apps.description: Minimal read-only UI for watching an Apteva agent — embed anywhere on the same host. Same-origin, path-mounted SPA; no sidecar.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: demo
apps.description: Client-facing demo runner. State board with live MCP-polled scenario cards, control rail with seed/reset recipes + scripted scenario triggers, chat dock for natural-language Q&A with the agent. Designed as a kiosk surface for client meetings.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: tasks
apps.description: Mission board for an Apteva instance. Agents create and complete tasks via MCP tools; the dashboard tracks progress live.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: todo
apps.description: Personal todo list — human-first counterpart to the agent mission board. Inbox / Today / Upcoming, projects, tags, priorities, due dates, snooze, and recurrence. Agent helps via natural-language quick-add, list, snooze, and complete tools.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: health
apps.description: Personal health log — weight, sleep, mood, heart rate, blood pressure, workouts. One flexible time-series table for any numeric metric plus a workouts list. Agent ingests natural-language one-liners ('weight 78.4', 'ran 5k 26min', 'bp 122/78') via health_log; the panel charts pinned kinds and lists recent sessions. v0.2 will add device sync (Apple Health, Oura, Garmin).
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: status
apps.description: Per-instance status line. Agent writes 'what I'm on right now' via MCP; dashboard reads it live. Short-horizon counterpart to the long-lived directive.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: crm
apps.description: Contacts store for Apteva agents and human teams. Multi-value channels, typed custom attributes with provenance, append-only activity log, soft-delete + merge.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: storage
apps.description: File storage with virtual folders, signed URLs, dedup. Pluggable backend: local disk by default, S3-compatible (AWS / R2 / B2 / Wasabi / MinIO) when configured. On S3, presigned upload + download URLs let clients transfer bytes directly to the bucket — bytes never proxy through this container.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: jobs
apps.description: Scheduled-job runner. Other apps (and agents) enqueue work to be delivered later — once at a future time, on an interval, or on a cron expression. At-least-once delivery with idempotency keys, exponential backoff, and a runs log.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: code
apps.description: Repositories — named, framework-tagged code workspaces scoped under Apteva projects. First-class editing tools modelled on Claude Code: partial reads with line numbers, exact-match string edits with uniqueness checks, atomic multi-edit, repo-wide grep + glob.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: media
apps.description: Catalog + derivations + renders + transcripts + auto-descriptions for audio/video/image files in storage. Pluggable render backend: ffmpeg by default, Cloudinary when bound (offload trim/resize/transcode/crop/extract_frame). Subscribes to storage events for instant indexing on upload + cascade cleanup on delete; falls back to a 30s safety-net poll. Smart video thumbnails skip black/fade openings.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: image-studio
apps.description: Generate images via any compatible provider (OpenAI today; Replicate / Stability / Fal as the catalog learns them). Optionally saves outputs to the Storage app for permanent, shareable references.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: calendar
apps.description: Self-contained calendar — multiple calendars per project, events with recurrence (rrule), holidays helper. Agents read + write via MCP tools; the panel renders week / day / agenda views with click-to-create. v0.2 will add public sharing + booking links + Google/Outlook sync.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: social
apps.description: Schedule and publish posts to your social media accounts (X, Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, Reddit, Pinterest, Threads). Connect accounts from inside the app — no separate integration setup. Posts can be triggered from the panel or by an agent via MCP tools.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: messaging
apps.description: Send and receive messages across channels (email via SES, SMS+WhatsApp via Twilio). v0.6 wires cross-app DNS automation: install the domains app + bind a registrar (Porkbun/Namecheap) → click 'Setup DNS' on a verified sender → DKIM CNAMEs + SES inbound MX + SPF are published automatically. v0.5 added full inbound (Twilio + SES verdicts/S3 + STOP suppression). Channel-agnostic addresses, per-channel suppression, local + provider-mirrored templates.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: domains
apps.description: DNS + domain inventory for Apteva projects. Other apps (messaging for SES MX/DKIM, storage for CDN CNAMEs) call this app for DNS record CRUD instead of speaking each registrar's API directly. v0.2 ships the full operator panel (add/remove domains, browse + add/edit/delete records inline) and Namecheap support via per-slug provider dispatch (XML response parsing + read-modify-write semantics). Porkbun and Namecheap both work end-to-end.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: deploy
apps.description: Local-first builds and runtime supervision for Apteva projects. Pluggable source kinds (code, local) and frameworks (go, static, blank) — fetches source, runs the framework toolchain, supervises the result as a child process bound to a local port. No Docker required for v0.1; future runtimes (Docker, SSH-to-VPS) plug in behind the same Runtime interface.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: channel-chat
apps.description: Chat bridge between the agent and the dashboard / external channels. Persists messages with monotonic ids for clean reconnect. Currently bundled with apteva-server as a built-in framework app; will be extracted to a standalone installable app in a future release.
apps.author: Apteva

apps.name: trading
apps.description: Trading desk for Apteva agents. Multi-portfolio, multi-asset (equity, crypto, polymarket). Each portfolio runs in paper mode (deterministic in-process engine) or live mode (orders route through a bound broker integration; v0.2 ships Binance only). Mandate, allowed asset classes, and daily-loss halt enforced server-side; halt cancels working broker orders upstream. Approval policy lives in the agent's directive, not the platform.
apps.author: Apteva
